title: Mackerel rundown
subtitle: Mackerel braised in coconut milk with scotch bonnet
excerpt: Mackerel fillets simmered in coconut milk with scotch bonnet, tomato, thyme, and spring onion until the coconut milk has reduced to a thick, fragrant sauce that clings to the fish. Rundown is the Jamaican technique of slowly cooking down coconut milk with fish or saltfish until it reaches a semi-solid consistency. Serve over boiled green banana or with rice.
sourceNotes: Rundown (also spelled rondón or run-dung) is a Jamaican coconut milk braise that appears across the Caribbean with regional variations. The name comes from the process of reducing the coconut milk until it 'runs down' to a thick sauce. Saltfish and mackerel are the traditional proteins; the dish appears in Jamaican culinary literature from the 19th century.

content.text: Full-fat tinned coconut milk is essential. Light coconut milk does not have enough fat to reduce properly and the sauce stays thin. Do not shake the tin before opening, tip it in undisturbed so the thick cream goes in with the watery liquid.

The scotch bonnet can be left whole and removed before serving for background heat, or sliced and left in for significant heat. Taste as you go. The coconut milk moderates the chilli considerably, a whole scotch bonnet in a full tin of coconut milk is warm rather than fierce.

Heat the {{vegetable-oil}} in a wide pan over medium heat. Add the {{onion}} and cook for 5 minutes until softened. Add the {{garlic}}, {{tomato}}, {{thyme}}, {{allspice}}, and {{scotch-bonnet}}. Cook for 3 minutes. Pour in the {{coconut-milk}} and bring to a steady simmer. Cook uncovered for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened and turned from white to a pale golden colour.

Season the {{mackerel-fillets}} with {{sea-salt-fine}} and {{black-pepper}}. Lay the fish pieces gently into the sauce. Add the {{spring-onion}}. Reduce the heat to low, cover, and cook for 8 to 10 minutes until the fish is cooked through and the sauce has reduced further to coat the fish thickly. Remove the scotch bonnet if left whole. Serve immediately over boiled green banana, rice, or with hard-dough bread.

items.fix: Remove the fish and increase the heat to reduce the sauce. The sauce should coat the back of a spoon before the fish goes in
items.cause: Coconut milk not reduced before fish added, or lid kept on throughout

items.fix: Mackerel cooks in 8 to 10 minutes at a low simmer. Shake the pan to distribute the sauce rather than stirring, which breaks up the fish
Keep the sauce at a steady simmer, not a rolling boil. Boiling causes the coconut fat to split from the liquid

content.text: Rundown is comfort food in the Jamaican tradition, cheap, quick, and deeply satisfying. It is made with whatever fish is available, from saltfish (the economical preserved version) to fresh mackerel at its best. The coconut milk base connects it to the broader Caribbean culinary tradition of one-pot coconut dishes that stretches from Jamaica through Trinidad to the islands of the Eastern Caribbean. In Jamaica it is most often eaten for breakfast or lunch, served over boiled ground provisions.
